pending.body: `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:176-189` only converts an idle milestone back to `ready` when the old snapshot status was already `active`. `update_task_run()` relies on that helper after sealing the lineage row (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:601-610`), while `record_bead_start()` still appends the running lineage entry before writing `status.json` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:458-480`). If the start path fails after the append but before the snapshot write, a later completion repair fixes the counters but leaves the milestone stuck in `planning` (or any other stale non-terminal status) even though a bead already ran and finished. Please make reconciliation derive the correct idle status from lineage for these partial-failure repairs, not only for the `active -> ready` case.

`record_bead_start()` is still not concurrency-safe for the idempotent retry case. It decides whether a running entry already exists from an unlocked `read_task_runs()` snapshot (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:387-423`) and only later performs a blind append (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:458-470`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:2428-2437`). The `BeadStarted` journal dedupe is another unlocked read-then-append (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:482-488`). Two controllers retrying the same `(bead_id, project_id, run_id)` concurrently can both observe “no existing row”, both append a running entry, and both emit start events. `update_task_run()` can collapse duplicate lineage rows later, but the duplicate journal history is permanent and snapshot counts are wrong until completion. The compare-and-insert for start idempotence needs to happen under a single lock.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 6 -> Plan and Implement
